request4_verdict: freeze MLRS as bounded observation-operator lab; hand off final weak-field posterior externally
primary_hand_off_target: PEP (if code acquisition / build is feasible)
secondary_hand_off_path: collaboration or external-analysis-center route via JPL / POLAC-ELPN
public_ephemeris_support_path: INPOP + CALCEPH or JPL DE + SPICE are support layers, not full fit-engine replacements

candidates.name: PEP (Planetary Ephemeris Program)
candidates.kind: full astrometric / ephemeris fit engine with LLR support
candidates.public_code_status: public-code candidate; paper says source/docs/tests are publicly available via GitLab
candidates.public_data_status: works with external ephemerides and heterogeneous astrometric data
candidates.rationale: Best external hand-off target found: it is described as an open-source general-purpose astrometric analysis program, its literature explicitly states lunar laser ranging support down to mm-class one-way precision, and ILRS literature shows it has already been used on real LLR data.

candidates.name: JPL Lunar Analysis Center / DE pipeline
candidates.kind: mature internal LLR analysis center workflow
candidates.public_code_status: ephemerides and reader toolkits public; full fit engine not identified as public
candidates.public_data_status: public DE ephemerides and SPICE readers available
candidates.rationale: Clearly mature and high-performing for LLR, but the public-facing assets are the DE ephemerides and access toolkits rather than a released end-to-end estimator. This is a credible collaboration / external-analysis route, not a drop-in public code hand-off.

candidates.name: POLAC / ELPN
candidates.kind: mature lunar analysis-center ephemeris and fit workflow
candidates.public_code_status: public analysis-center capability; no public code path identified in bounded scout
candidates.public_data_status: public predictions and analysis-center outputs described
candidates.rationale: Strong science maturity and explicit use for LLR fundamental-physics fits, including variational equations and SME-style extensions, but the bounded scout did not identify a public code release suitable as a local estimator drop-in.

candidates.name: INPOP + CALCEPH
candidates.kind: public ephemerides + open-source ephemeris access layer
candidates.llr_fit_engine: not in public code found here
candidates.public_code_status: reader libraries public; fit engine not identified as public
candidates.public_data_status: ephemeris files public; Moon residual computation offered to users
candidates.integration_effort: medium for ephemeris side, high for full posterior
candidates.rationale: Useful if the project needs a public ephemeris / residual-computation substrate, but the public assets found are the ephemeris products and CALCEPH readers, not a released full LLR estimator.

candidates.name: EPM / IAA RAS
candidates.kind: mature ephemeris and web residual service
candidates.public_data_status: ephemerides and web O-C services public
candidates.rationale: Scientifically mature and explicitly active in LLR residual analysis, but the official site states that the scientific software engine is not distributed, so this is not a local code hand-off candidate.

interpretation: PEP is the strongest public-code candidate found for an actual LLR-capable estimator hand-off.
JPL and POLAC are scientifically mature but were not identified here as public drop-in estimator releases.
INPOP/CALCEPH and JPL DE/SPICE are valuable ephemeris/residual-access layers but not sufficient by themselves to replace a full weak-field estimator fit engine.
IAA RAS EPM is mature but the official site states the engine is not distributed.
